claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 3 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 8 years old and does not live with you.
  - Charlie is 10 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 18 weeks but claimant regularly spends on child
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the official eligibility rules so I can guide you accurately.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. To help me assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have any children you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I have three children - Alex, Blake, and Charlie.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, Blake, and Charlie, I need to understand more about each child.

Let me start with their ages. How old are Alex, Blake, and Charlie?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is 3, Blake is 8, and Charlie is 10.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Now I need to understand your living arrangements with each child.

Do Alex, Blake, and Charlie all live with you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex and Charlie live with me, but Blake doesn't live with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. Since Blake doesn't live with you, I need to understand if you're still contributing to their upkeep.

Are you contributing financially towards Blake's care (such as for food, clothes, pocket money, or accommodation)? If so, how much do you contribute?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not currently contributing financially towards Blake's care.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Now I need to understand a bit more about your own circumstances.

Do you currently live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One final question to complete my assessment:

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, Blake, or Charlie?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for any of them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me now assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for each of your three children.

Based on the information you've provided:

- **Alex (age 3)**: Lives with you in the UK, is under 16, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them.
- **Blake (age 8)**: Does not live with you, and you are not contributing financially towards their care.
- **Charlie (age 10)**: Lives with you in the UK, is under 16, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Summary of Your Child Benefit Eligibility

Based on your circumstances, here's the outcome for each of your children:

**✓ Alex (age 3) - ELIGIBLE**
You can claim Child Benefit for Alex because they are under 16, live with you in the UK, and nobody else is claiming for them.

**✗ Blake (age 8) - NOT ELIGIBLE**
You cannot claim Child Benefit for Blake because they don't live with you and you're not making financial contributions towards their care. To be eligible for a child who doesn't live with you, you would need to contribute at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent) towards their upkeep (such as food, clothes, or pocket money).

**✓ Charlie (age 10) - ELIGIBLE**
You can claim Child Benefit for Charlie because they are under 16, live with you in the UK, and nobody else is claiming for them.

**What this means:** You can apply for Child Benefit for Alex and Charlie. If you wanted to claim for Blake in the future, you would need to either have Blake live with you or start contributing financially towards their care at a level equivalent to at least the Child Benefit amount.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about your Child Benefit eligibility?

reasonings: Alex is 3 years old (under 16), lives with you in the UK, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit. You are responsible for Alex as they live with you. According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. Therefore, you are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 8 years old (under 16) but does not live with you. You are not making any financial contributions towards Blake's upkeep. According to the eligibility rules, you'll usually be responsible for a child if either you live with them OR you're paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit towards looking after them (e.g., on food, clothes or pocket money). Since Blake doesn't live with you and you're not contributing financially, you do not meet the responsibility criteria. Therefore, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Blake.

Charlie is 10 years old (under 16), lives with you in the UK, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit. You are responsible for Charlie as they live with you. According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. Therefore, you are eligible for Child Benefit for Charlie.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester) with two of their three children. They are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex (age 3) and Charlie (age 10) as both children live with them, are under 16, and nobody else is claiming for them. However, they are not eligible for Blake (age 8) because Blake does not live with them and the claimant is not making any financial contributions towards Blake's care, meaning they do not meet the responsibility criteria for that child.
